name: Mexican Rabbit
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the rabbit, parsley, rosemary, thyme, bay leaf, and peppercorns in a large saucepan. Add enough water to cover.
2. Bring to a boil and simmer for 30 minutes or until the rabbit is tender. Drain the rabbit and reserve 1 cup of liquid.
3. Remove the rabbit from the bones.
4. Sauté the garlic and shallots in a small amount of fat in a skillet until tender, then add the rabbit, corn, tomatoes, olives, chili powder, salt, and pepper.
5. Simmer for 20 minutes.
6. Combine the cornmeal and egg yolk. Strain the reserved liquid and add it to the cornmeal mixture. Mix well.
7. Stir into the rabbit mixture and spoon into a greased casserole. Bake at 325 degrees for 40 minutes or until set. Serves 6.
